name: An Emergent Mirage: Is Emergent Misalignment and Realignment Indeed a Robust Phenomenon?
content: Recent work has reported Emergent Misalignment (EM), where language models fine-tuned on narrow, domain-specific misaligned datasets abruptly acquire broadly misaligned behavior, alongside evidence that this behavior can be reversed through limited realignment. We systematically study repeated alignment and misalignment cycles using controlled fine-tuning loops while tracking behavioral performance, and LoRA representations throughout training. Although we reproduce EM, we find that both misalig
summary: 通过控制细调循环并跟踪训练过程中行为表现和LoRA表示，我们验证了Emergent Misalignment（EM）现象的存在，并发现其敏感依赖于数据集的表面特征。此外，先前报告的机制性签名并未与行为上的不一致相关联。
key_insights: EM 现象在控制了响应长度差异后仍存在显著变化
EM 的敏感度高，尤其是对数据集的表面特征依赖
先前报道的机制性签名未能与行为上的不一致性相关联
xihe_relevance: 有助于理解语言模型的行为模式及其可解释性
